| | | |
|:-------|:-------:|-------:|
| [Previous Class](../class_6/narratives_6.qmd) | [List of Classes](../../catalog.qmd) | [Next Class](../class_8/narratives_8.qmd) |
:  {tbl-colwidths="[33,33,34]"}

# Class 7 Assignment Examples{.ct}

![](../../graphics/REB_Logo.png){fig-alt="Reaction Engineering Basics Logo" fig-align="center" width="200"}

## Preparation Example {.ch}

**REB, the Book, Example 4.8.6** The oxidation of carbon monoxide, reaction (1) is heterogeneously catalyzed. A proposed reaction mechanism is shown in reactions (2) through (6). Assume that step (6) is rate-limiting and derive an expression for the apparent rate of reaction (1) that contains only partial pressures, rate coefficients and equilibrium constants. How does the apparent rate expression change if $O\!-\!\ast$ is the most abundant surface intermediate?

**Overall Reaction:**

$$
2 CO + O_2 \rightleftarrows 2 CO_2 \tag{1}
$$

**Proposed Mechanism:**
$$
O_2 + \ast \rightleftarrows O_2\!-\!\ast \tag{2}
$$

$$
CO + O_2\!-\!\ast \rightleftarrows CO_3\!-\!\ast \tag{3}
$$

$$
CO_3\!-\!\ast \rightleftarrows CO_2 + O\!-\!\ast \tag{4}
$$

$$
CO + O\!-\!\ast \rightleftarrows CO_2\!-\!\ast \tag{5}
$$

$$
CO_2\!-\!\ast \rightleftarrows CO_2 + \ast \tag{6}
$$

[Example 4.8.6 Solution](Example%204.8.6.pdf)

## Learning Activity {.ch}

Reaction (1) is non-elementary and is catalyzed in liquid solution by C. The mechanism is shown in reactions (2), (3) and (4), all of which are reversible. Derive an expression for the apparent rate of reaction (1) that is based upon the mechanism and assumes that C-I is the most abundant intermediate. Your rate expression may contain the initial concentration of the catalyst, but not the concentration of C, C-I or I.

$$
A + B \rightleftarrows Y + Z \tag{1}
$$

$$
C + A \rightleftarrows C\mbox{-}I + Y \tag{2}
$$

$$
C\mbox{-}I \rightleftarrows C + I \tag{3}
$$

$$
C\mbox{-}I + B \rightleftarrows C + Z \tag{4}
$$

[Learning Activity Solution](Activity%207.pdf)

## Practice Assignment {.ch} 

Suppose that the non-elementary conversion of substrate to product, equation (1) is enzymatic and requires a cofactor, C. The concentration of the cofactor is easy to measure. The mechanism of the reaction is given by equations (2) through (4), where step (4) is effectively irreversible. Derive an expression for the apparent rate of enzymatic conversion of substrate to product, and show how the apparent rate expression changes if the enzyme-cofactor complex (E-C) is the most abundant intermediate.

$$
S \rightarrow P \tag{1}
$$

$$
E + C \rightleftarrows E\mbox{-}C \tag{2}
$$

$$
E\mbox{-}C + S \rightleftarrows S\mbox{-}E\mbox{-}C \tag{3}
$$

$$
S\mbox{-}E\mbox{-}C \rightarrow E\mbox{-}C + P \tag{4}
$$

[Class 7 Practice Assignment Solution](Practice%20Assignment%207.pdf)

## Supplemental Examples {.ch}

[to be added]

{{< include ../../includes/_bottom_license.qmd >}}
